skill.name: ralph-tui-create-json
skill.description: Convert product requirement documents into flat prd.json task files for ralph-tui execution.
skill.summary: Create ralph-tui task JSON from PRDs with stories, criteria, dependencies, and quality gates.
skill.author: subsy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown code spans as shell execution and weak cryptography. Review found no executable scripts, no cryptographic code, no network access, and no prompt injection attempt. The remaining risk is low because the skill may preserve quality-gate command text for later review or execution by another tool.

low_findings.title: Command Text Propagated Into Task Criteria
low_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to copy quality-gate command text such as package manager checks into generated acceptance criteria. This is not direct execution, but users should review commands before any downstream tool runs them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains command text in Markdown examples, but no code path executes it. Risk depends on a downstream agent or user choosing to run generated quality gates.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: Static high findings for weak cryptography were reviewed as false positives. The cited lines are Markdown front matter, prose, or JSON examples, with no hashing or encryption implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is documentation and sample JSON, not executable cryptographic code. No evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak algorithm use was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static medium findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution were reviewed as false positives. The backticks mark inline code and fenced examples in Markdown, not Ruby execution syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only source file is SKILL.md, and the detected backticks occur in Markdown prose and examples. No Ruby file, script block, or executable shell wrapper exists.

content.user_title: Convert PRDs Into Ralph TUI JSON Tasks
content.value_statement: Teams often write PRDs that are too broad for autonomous implementation loops. This skill converts PRD content into structured ralph-tui stories with dependencies and quality gates.
actual_capabilities: Extracts quality gates from PRD text or asks for them when missing.
Creates flat prd.json structures with root-level name and userStories fields.
Breaks PRD scope into small user stories for ralph-tui iterations.
Adds acceptance criteria, priorities, passes flags, notes fields, and dependencies.
Appends UI verification gates to stories that need browser review.
Identifies schema anti-patterns that would break ralph-tui loading.
limitations: It does not execute ralph-tui or validate the generated file with a runtime parser.
It depends on clear PRD input for accurate story boundaries.
It cannot guarantee that all downstream quality gate commands are safe.
It is focused on the JSON tracker format, not other tracker plugins.

use_cases.title: Prepare Autonomous Coding Runs
use_cases.description: Convert a feature PRD into ordered stories that ralph-tui can process one iteration at a time.

use_cases.title: Normalize Task Planning
use_cases.description: Turn inconsistent markdown requirements into a consistent task structure with acceptance criteria and dependencies.

use_cases.title: Add Quality Gates
use_cases.description: Attach required checks and UI verification steps to each story before implementation begins.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a PRD
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into ralph-tui prd.json format. Keep stories small, add quality gates, and include dependencies.

prompt_templates.title: Create Tasks From File
prompt_templates.scenario: Use an existing PRD markdown file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the PRD in tasks/prd.md and create tasks/prd.json for ralph-tui. Ask me for quality gates if the PRD does not list them.

prompt_templates.title: Review Story Size
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a draft task plan before saving.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PRD task breakdown for ralph-tui. Split oversized stories and make acceptance criteria verifiable.

prompt_templates.title: Model Dependencies
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced dependency planning for multi-step implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PRD into ralph-tui tasks with careful dependency ordering. Put schema work first, then backend, UI, and integration work.

output_examples.input: A PRD for adding priority levels to a task app.
output_examples.output: A ralph-tui task file with database, UI badge, and filter stories ordered by dependency.

output_examples.input: A PRD that lists typecheck, lint, and browser verification gates.
output_examples.output: Stories include the required checks, with browser verification added only to UI-related work.

output_examples.input: A broad dashboard PRD with several unrelated features.
output_examples.output: The work is split into smaller stories for schema, queries, components, filters, and final integration.

best_practices: Provide the full PRD and the intended quality gates before conversion.
Review generated dependencies before running autonomous implementation.
Keep each story small enough for one ralph-tui iteration.
anti_patterns: Do not wrap the result in extra root objects.
Do not combine many unrelated features into one story.
Do not run generated quality gate commands without reviewing them.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a ralph-tui compatible prd.json task structure from PRD text or a PRD file.

faq.question: Does it run ralph-tui?
faq.answer: No. It prepares the JSON task file and shows how ralph-tui can use it.

faq.question: Can it work without quality gates?
faq.answer: Yes. It should ask for missing gates or use a sensible project default.

faq.question: Does it support tracker plugins beyond JSON?
faq.answer: No. The documented format targets the ralph-tui JSON tracker plugin.

faq.question: How does it set dependencies?
faq.answer: It orders stories from foundational changes to integration work and adds dependsOn references where needed.

faq.question: Is generated command text executed automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill documents command text for acceptance criteria. Review commands before any downstream tool runs them.
